title: Translate Requests into Testable User Goals
summary: Build and verify design user goals in community survey redesign discovery interview through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain design user goals from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply design user goals without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim preserves design user goals when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: community survey redesign discovery interview.

options.text: Community survey redesign discovery interview case 1: the stakeholder feature list is equivalent to evidence about user needs.

Community survey redesign discovery interview case 1: separate requests from research evidence, state prioritized user tasks and constraints, and define observable success before choosing interface features.
Community survey redesign discovery interview case 1: treat every requested feature as equally urgent so no stakeholder preference is challenged.

steps.title: 04 · Read the community survey redesign discovery interview case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves design user goals when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: community survey redesign discovery interview.
steps.why: Translate Requests into Testable User Goals makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in community survey redesign discovery interview.

content.text: A stakeholder request is an input, not proof of a user need. A design brief names people, jobs, contexts, constraints, risks, assumptions, and observable success evidence. Prioritized goals prevent a feature list from competing equally and let the team test whether a resident can complete the actual service task. Apply it to community survey redesign discovery interview.

options.text: Community survey redesign discovery interview case 5: keep the community survey redesign discovery interview defect because the happy path worked once.

Community survey redesign discovery interview case 5: a plausible success state is incomplete evidence. Separate requests from research evidence, state prioritized user tasks and constraints, and define observable success before choosing interface features.
Community survey redesign discovery interview case 5: replace the complete artifact before isolating the failed relationship.
